page: The UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-3xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for intensive parallel processing workloads. It features 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, with 32 physical cores and a single thread per core configuration. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per core. Graphics and parallel computing acceleration are delivered by three NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing a combined total of 144 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local storage, meaning external storage volumes must be attached, and it comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier performance across both compute and memory operations. The AMD EPYC CPU achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core tests, as well as in PassMark CPU physics and extended instruction tests. Memory performance is also strong, with read-only cached small block bandwidth reaching 6377.89 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 123.17 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25%. Database and web serving capabilities are highly rated, with Redis SET operations executing at 128.07 million operations per second and static web serving throughput reaching 97.51 GB/sec.

Given the high density of GPU resources and system memory, this server type offers a specialized hardware profile where cost efficiency is realized through the consolidation of heavy parallel workloads. The absence of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring users to provision network-attached storage separately. This configuration is highly suited for GPU-accelerated workloads such as deep learning, rendering, large-scale data science, and complex simulations, as well as high-throughput database operations and parallelized software compilation.
description: The UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-3xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server designed for high-performance parallel computing. It features 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor, 384.0 GB of system memory, and three NVIDIA L40S GPUs with a total of 144 GB of VRAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under KVM virtualization, the server lacks local storage but includes one public IPv4 address. Benchmark results place this instance in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, with strong memory bandwidth and database processing capabilities. This hardware profile is optimized for parallel processing tasks, including machine learning, rendering, and scientific simulations, where massive GPU acceleration and high memory capacity are required.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-3xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and three NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 144 GB VRAM, optimized for parallel computing and data-intensive workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-3xL40S features 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and three NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 144 GB VRAM for high-performance parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-performance parallel computing instance featuring triple NVIDIA L40S GPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 32 AMD EPYC processor cores.
bullet_points: Three NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 144 GB total VRAM for parallel acceleration.
32 AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs delivering top-tier single and multi-core performance.
384 GB of system memory providing 12 GB of RAM per core.
No local storage included, requiring external volume attachment.
Best suited for deep learning, rendering, and high-throughput database operations.
